content_markdown:
A wave of period $5.0\text{ ms}$ travels through a medium. The graph shows the variation with distance $d$ of the displacement $x$ of points in the medium.

What is the average speed of a point in the medium during one full oscillation?

diagram_metadata.description: A displacement-distance graph showing a sinusoidal wave. The vertical axis is displacement x in cm, ranging from -2 to 2. The horizontal axis is distance d in m, ranging from 0 to 4. The wave has a peak at d=0, a trough at d=1, a peak at d=2, a trough at d=3, and a peak at d=4.

vibe_explanation: To find the average speed of a point in the medium, we look at the total distance traveled by a single particle in one period. From the graph, the amplitude $A$ is $2\text{ cm}$ ($0.02\text{ m}$). In one full oscillation, a particle moves from equilibrium to max positive displacement, back to equilibrium, to max negative displacement, and back to equilibrium—a total distance of $4A = 4 \times 2\text{ cm} = 8\text{ cm} = 0.08\text{ m}$. The time taken for one oscillation is the period $T = 5.0\text{ ms} = 0.005\text{ s}$. Average speed is $\text{distance} / \text{time} = 0.08\text{ m} / 0.005\text{ s} = 16\text{ m s}^{-1}$.
